commands.review_summary:
The PR passes Telegram `msg.from.is_bot` through `SenderFacts`, exposes `SenderIsBot` in `MsgContext`, renders `is_bot` in inbound sender metadata, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +7. Total +11 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows Telegram has access to `msg.from.is_bot` and `SenderFacts.isBot`, but the Telegram builder, finalized context, and prompt metadata do not expose it before this PR.

target.title: fix(telegram): expose sender bot status in context
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #40838. Telegram inbound messages already carry `msg.from.is_bot`, and the shared channel sender model already has `SenderFacts.isBot`, but the value was not projected into the finalized OpenClaw message context or the sender metadata shown to the agent.

## Why This Change Was Made

Telegram users need agent-visible sender bot status to distinguish human senders from bot-authored messages without relying on fragile name or username heuristics. This keeps the fix narrow by reusing the existing sender facts path instead of adding raw Telegram payloads to prompts.

## User Impact

Agents handling Telegram turns can now see `SenderIsBot` in the runtime message context, and the untrusted sender metadata block includes `is_bot` when Telegram provides that boolean. Existing sender id/name/username behavior is unchanged.

## Real Behavior Proof

I ran a redacted terminal proof on this PR head. The proof uses the patched shared inbound context and prompt metadata path with a Telegram-shaped sender from `msg.from.is_bot: true`, then parses the rendered sender metadata block.

```json
{
  "proof": "telegram sender bot status reaches agent context and prompt metadata",
  "ctxPayload": {
    "SenderId": "42",
    "SenderUsername": "proof_bot",
    "SenderIsBot": true
  },
  "senderMetadata": {
    "id": "42",
    "username": "proof_bot",
    "is_bot": true
  }
}
```

I also reran the target regression tests on this PR head; the Telegram shard includes the patched transport builder assertion that `from.is_bot` is passed as `sender.isBot` and projected as `SenderIsBot`.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-topic-threadid.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `pnpm oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/templating.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-topic-threadid.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
